title: dryopteris filix-mas var. rosthornii
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas var. rosthornii, commonly known as Chinese fern, is a rare variety of the Dryopteris filix-mas plant, a type of evergreen fern native to China. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. The therapeutic actions of Dryopteris filix-mas var. rosthornii include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which contribute to its healing effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

parts: Dryopteris filix-mas var. rosthornii has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, which are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, and also contain phenolic acids like ferulic acid. The rhizome of the plant is another important medicinal part, containing compounds like aucubin and caffeic acid, which may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The roots of Dryopteris filix-mas var. rosthornii contain saponins, which may be used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. side_effects: Dryopteris filix-mas var. rosthornii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications.
